author: śrīla raghunāthadāsa gosvāmī
verses:
vṛṣabha⋅danuja⋅nāśān narma⋅dharmokti⋅raṅgair
nikhila⋅nija⋅sakhībhir jat sva⋅hastena pūrṇam
prakaṭitam api vṛndāraṇya⋅rājñyā pramodais
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

vraja⋅bhuvi mura⋅śatroḥ preyasīnāṁ nikāmair
asulabham api tūrṇaṁ prema⋅kalpa⋅drumaṁ tam
janayati hṛdi bhūmau snātur uccaiḥ priyaṁ jat
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

agha⋅ripur api jatnād atra devyāḥ prasāda⋅
prasara⋅kṛta⋅kaṭākṣa⋅prāpti⋅kāmaḥ prakāmam
anusarati jad uccaiḥ snāna⋅sevānu⋅bandhais
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

vraja⋅bhuvana⋅sudhāṁśoḥ prema⋅bhūmir nikāmaṁ
vraja⋅madhura⋅kiśorī⋅mauli⋅ratna⋅priyeva
paricitam api nāmnā jac ca tenaiva tasyās
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

api jana iha kaścid jasya sevā⋅prasādaiḥ
praṇaya⋅sura⋅latā syāt tasya goṣṭhendra⋅sūnoḥ
sapadi kila mad⋅īśā⋅dāsya⋅puṣpa⋅praśasyā
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

taṭa⋅madhura⋅nikuñjāḥ klṛpta⋅nāmāna uccair
nija⋅parijana⋅vargaiḥ saṁvibhajyāśritās taiḥ
madhukara⋅ruta⋅ramyā jasya rājanti kāmyās
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

taṭa⋅bhuvi vara⋅vedyāṁ jasya narmāti⋅hṛdyāṁ
madhura⋅madhura⋅vārtāṁ goṣṭha⋅candrasya bhaṅgyā
prathayati mitha īśā prāṇa⋅sakhyālibhiḥ sā
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

anudinam ati⋅raṅgaiḥ prema⋅mattāli⋅saṅghair
vara⋅sarasija⋅gandhair hāri⋅vāri⋅prapūrṇe
viharata iha jasmin dam⋅patī tau pramattau
tad ati⋅surabhi⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam evāśrayo me

avikalam ati devyāś cāru kuṇḍāṣṭakaṁ jaḥ
paripaṭhati tadīyollāsi⋅dāsyārpitātmā
aciram iha śarīre darśayaty eva tasmai
madhu⋅ripur ati⋅modaiḥ śliṣyamāṇāṁ priyāṁ tāma
en_translation:
After the slaying of the bull demon, when Rādhā and Her gopī friends filled this pond with Their own hands after a playful dispute with Lord Kṛṣṇa, and even though it was manifested with great joy by the Queen of Vṛndāvana herself—that extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

In the sacred land of Vraja, for the dearest beloveds of Lord Kṛṣṇa, this pond quickly generates the desire-tree of divine love in the heart of anyone who bathes here. To that soul it is exceedingly dear. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

Even the enemy of the Agha demon, Lord Kṛṣṇa, intensely desires to attain the merciful side-long glance of Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī here and eagerly follows Her while performing the service of bathing. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

This is intensely the land of love for the moon of Vraja, Lord Kṛṣṇa, and it is as dear to Him as the crown-jewel of the gopīs herself. Even though known by Her Name, it is intimately connected to Her. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

If any person here, by the grace of serving this pond, may quickly have the creeper of divine love for the Son of Nanda Mahārāja manifest in his heart—service which is praised as the flower of devotion to my Mistress. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

The sweet groves on its banks, given beautiful names by Her own associates, are divided and sheltered by them. These groves are charming with the humming of bees and shine with desirable features. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

On the beautiful platform on its bank, the Mistress reveals sweet and charming topics concerning the moon of Vraja among Her dearest friends. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

Daily, with great delight and accompanied by the gopīs who are intoxicated with love, the Divine Couple sports here in the captivating waters filled with the fragrance of lotuses. That extremely fragrant Rādhā-kuṇḍa alone is my refuge.

Whoever flawlessly recites these eight verses about the beautiful pond of the Goddess, with a soul dedicated to Her joyful service—the enemy of Madhu will very soon, even in this very body, reveal to him his Beloved Rādhā as He embraces Her with great joy.
